items.headline: U.S. Markets Rally on Peace Hopes; Fed Meeting Underway
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed significantly higher on Monday, extending gains into Tuesday's pre-market, driven by optimism over a preliminary peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The deal eased Middle East tensions and sent oil prices lower, boosting investor sentiment. The Federal Reserve's two-day June meeting commenced today, with expectations for interest rates to remain unchanged amidst ongoing inflation concerns.

items.headline: SpaceX continues post-IPO ascent
items.summary: Shares of Space Exploration Technologies extended their post-IPO rally on Tuesday, adding another 9% to reach $209.57 per share. This builds on a 19.6% gain from Monday and its strong June 12 debut.

items.headline: Western Digital surges on data-center demand
items.summary: Western Digital Corporation saw significant pre-market gains, rising over 10% on bullish analyst comments regarding data-center storage demand. The stock had also surged 16.1% on Monday.

items.headline: Qualcomm boosted by AI device plans
items.summary: Qualcomm shares gained after CEO Cristiano Amon detailed the company's strategic push into next-generation AI-powered consumer devices, revealing over 40 product designs in progress.

items.headline: Rogers Communications dips in pre-market
items.summary: Rogers Communications Inc. was among the top pre-market losers on Tuesday.

items.headline: NRG Energy sees pre-market decline
items.summary: NRG Energy, Inc. experienced a notable drop in Tuesday's pre-market trading.

items.headline: Oil prices weigh on USO fund
items.summary: The United States Oil Fund LP fell as oil prices continued their downward trend following reports of a peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran, which eased concerns about energy turmoil.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Begins June Meeting; Rates Expected to Hold Steady
items.summary: The Federal Reserve commenced its two-day June meeting on Tuesday, June 16. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is presiding over his first FOMC meeting, and the market widely anticipates the federal funds rate will remain unchanged due to persistent inflation concerns.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released Ahead of Fed Decision
items.summary: The NY Empire State Manufacturing Index for June fell significantly to 5.7, missing expectations. Industrial production for May rose by a modest 0.1%, while capacity utilization came in slightly below estimates at 76.2%. Investors are also awaiting U.S. housing starts and trade price data later today.

items.headline: Tech and Communication Services Lead Rally; Energy Lags
items.summary: On Monday, the Information Technology ($XLK) and Communication Services ($XLC) sectors were top performers, rising 3.4% and 2.4% respectively, reflecting strong sentiment around AI and chip-linked names. In contrast, the Energy ($XLE) sector lagged significantly, falling 3.6% as crude oil prices continued to decline amidst geopolitical de-escalation.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Released; Waterdrop Posts Strong Growth
items.summary: Several companies are scheduled to report earnings today, including John Wiley & Sons ($WLY) and La-Z-Boy ($LZB). Waterdrop Inc. ($WDH) announced robust first-quarter 2026 unaudited financial results, with net operating revenue increasing 64.8% year-over-year to $180.1 million and operating profit up 5.3%.
